description: Sector overlay for U.S. money services businesses, payment processors, remittance providers, prepaid products, digital wallets, and payment-service documentation.

metadata.scope: United States sector overlay controls for Payment Platforms and Services. Intended to be used with us/core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from U.S. federal statutes, regulations, agency guidance, regulator materials, and sector frameworks available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance. State, territorial, tribal, and local obligations may impose additional requirements.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: FinCEN money services business and BSA/AML materials
CFPB remittance transfer and prepaid account rules
Federal Reserve Regulation E / Electronic Fund Transfer Act materials
NACHA Operating Rules and ACH network materials

rules.title: MSB registration, BSA/AML program, or money-transmission evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for FinCEN MSB registration, AML program, money transmitter, suspicious activity reporting, or BSA recordkeeping evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Consumer payment disclosures and error-resolution controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for Regulation E, electronic fund transfer disclosures, error resolution, unauthorized transfer, prepaid account, or remittance evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: CFPB Regulation E and remittance/prepaid-account materials

rules.title: ACH authorization and network-rule evidence is present where ACH is described
rules.summary: Checks for ACH authorization, NACHA rules, ODFI/RDFI, return rates, account validation, or transaction monitoring evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Nacha Operating Rules and ACH network materials

rules.title: Payment data security and access-control evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for PCI DSS, encryption, tokenization, access control, incident response, or payment-data security evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified U.S. legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, federal/state interaction, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
